creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: This talk presents a layered approach to maintaining brand voice in AI systems, breaking voice into four components: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach draws on production experience from wedding venue, personal companion, and missing persons applications to explain why single-pass prompt instructions fail at scale.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present a system for converting a large personal knowledge vault into actionable context for AI agents. The approach uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, indexed layer, wiki layer) to efficiently organize thousands of notes and documents, enabling deep research queries across personal and web sources without requiring vector or graph databases.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov discusses building and scaling the OG Assist AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The talk covers agent architecture, core loops, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, tooling, and integration with Claude and Cursor.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory to be effective, using an analogy of John Carmack unable to see the full codebase. He frames this as a critical limitation that needs solving and positions Nx and Polygraph as solutions for improving agent autonomy in monorepo environments.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model choice accounts for only 10% of outcomes while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) determines the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, context engineering as core skill, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is an encrypted memory layer that persists across different AI agent providers, allowing users to maintain context when switching between tools like Claude, Codex, and Pi. The system encrypts memories before storage and gives users control over which agents can access which memories.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The reviewer Cole Medin highlights its strong performance for its size and cost.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: The video explains how AI engineers use self-prompting agent loops instead of direct prompting, where an orchestrator spawns worker agents in parallel to execute tasks. The approach enables hands-off automation but introduces cost and hallucination compounding challenges that require observability harnesses and deterministic workflow controls.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenClaw Creator's new project involving Crabbox and Codebase harness setup tools available on GitHub. The project includes skills for configuration and is promoted through AI Builder Club and Superdesign platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer and provides setup guidance for building AI agents. The video includes links to a GitHub repository with a codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving systems through autonomous feedback loops. It covers use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and discusses tools and memory mechanisms that enable this approach.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding capabilities, including the /goal feature for defining coding tasks and mission exploration for long-horizon task execution. The video covers practical setup and prompting strategies for autonomous coding agents and positions this as the next evolution in AI-assisted development.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code provides a technical walkthrough of how Generative Pretrained Transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video explains that GPT architectures power leading models including Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code provides a 10-minute breakdown of Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model explanation, covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization. The video serves as a pedagogical foundation for understanding more complex models like GPT, emphasizing key concepts such as cross-entropy, softmax, and backpropagation.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and Mixture of Tokens for efficient inference and performance.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. The model incorporates tiled improvements and KV cache optimizations that allow continuous single reads, reducing memory bottlenecks during both prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS tutorial introduces a framework for system design interviews using seven steps: requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes problem-first thinking rather than technology-first design.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling is a technique where AI agents request external tools to execute actions and retrieve real-time data, moving beyond pure language prediction. The agent receives a tool schema and description, then decides when to invoke it based on system prompts, creating a loop of model requests and framework execution. This transforms a chatbot that guesses into an agent that accesses live information and takes actions.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and guardrails for working with AWS services. It improves agent success rates and reduces inefficient operations by providing verified procedures and current knowledge instead of relying on outdated general information.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS walkthrough on running Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Bedrock Guardrails for content governance.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing whether Google engineers use code generation tools like OpenAI Codex and Claude Code. The video compares code generation adoption across companies and examines trends at Google from 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company perceived as a threat to open source development. The video explores how Malus operates and its implications for the future of open source maintainers, framing it as a potential disruption to the open source model.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats are built on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that the best AI products create raving fans and that fears of AI disrupting SaaS are exaggerated.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a humanoid robotics race led by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision. She contends that the real challenge in robotics is not intelligence but rather designing robots for human interaction and delight, which will be essential as billions of robots enter the world.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against much larger enterprise incumbents by building a full platform from day one rather than starting with a single feature. She argues that even innovators like Google relied on spreadsheets for strategic planning because existing solutions don't scale, and shares her hiring philosophy and growth strategy centered on brand trust and credibility signals.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early adopters do not signal real product-market fit and that founders should embed with customers in their daily workflows rather than conduct formal interviews. He emphasizes that genuine PMF manifests as customer pull (pricing inquiry) rather than push, and advises betting on AI model trajectories while avoiding self-deluding narratives.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof warns that agentic AI poses overlooked security threats including prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions. Businesses are hesitant to adopt agentic AI systems due to these risks, despite the technology's potential.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: The "This Is Tech Year" podcast features discussions about Anthropic's latest developments hosted by Cyril Imhof. The episode includes guest Joao and references to cultural events in Lisbon.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: A comparison of productivity and meeting overhead between small and large teams. The commentary argues that smaller agile teams accomplish tasks more efficiently while larger teams become bogged down in meetings and coordination overhead.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The AI boom is driving demand for high bandwidth memory (HBM). Micron and SK Hynix are positioned as key players shaping the future of memory infrastructure for AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
